disclaimer: Groundwork does not handle donations or allocate money. These links go directly to independent organizations. Listing is not endorsement or a ranking; see actions/README.md for the curation policy.

resources.note: The largest humanitarian food-assistance organization, operating worldwide.

resources.title: Donate to Action Against Hunger
resources.note: Hunger treatment and prevention programs in 55+ countries.

resources.title: Send cash directly to people in extreme poverty
resources.note: Unconditional cash transfers with published evidence base.

resources.title: Built for Zero — end homelessness measurably
resources.note: By-name data + Housing First; participating communities have measurably ended veteran or chronic homelessness. The closest thing to a proven playbook for 'ending' a need category.

resources.title: Back Housing First policy
resources.note: Housing First has the strongest evidence base for ending chronic homelessness — stable housing first, services after.

resources.title: Advocate to protect and expand SNAP
resources.note: For every meal a food bank provides, SNAP provides ~nine. Policy is the biggest anti-hunger lever in the US.

resources.title: Train as a volunteer poverty-policy advocate
resources.note: Grassroots advocacy on EITC/CTC and global nutrition — policies with measured poverty effects (the 2021 expanded CTC roughly halved US child poverty while active).

resources.title: Push universal school meals and full SNAP participation
resources.note: Closing the SNAP and school-meal participation gap reaches millions already eligible — the largest unclaimed anti-hunger resource in the US.

resources.title: Scale interventions proven by randomized trials
resources.note: Runs only programs with RCT-grade evidence and publishes its failures; reaches hundreds of millions at single-digit dollars per person.

resources.title: Fund the research that finds what actually ends poverty
resources.note: 600+ randomized evaluations of poverty programs — the evidence base the other levers stand on.

resources.title: Give where the evidence says it goes furthest
resources.note: Independent, transparent cost-effectiveness research on global-health and poverty charities.

resources.title: FAO — global food security data and programmes
resources.note: The source institution behind the undernourishment baseline shown on the world map.
